concept_description.value: Comprehensive half-day preschool with option to offer private kindergarten; Reggio Emilia-aligned (NAREA membership required); NAEYC accreditation required after two years of operation

parents_and_affiliates.value: On August 29, 2025, Ivybrook OpCo LLC (Delaware LLC, wholly owned subsidiary of Ivybrook HoldCo, LLC, which is a wholly owned subsidiary of Crux I Ivybrook (Aggregator), LP — a Delaware LP headquartered at 3838 Oak Lawn Ave #1500, Dallas, TX 75219) acquired all issued and outstanding membership interests in Ivybrook Franchising, LLC and its affiliates McWilliams Education Services, LLC and Catapult Industries, LLC. OpCo was formed August 15, 2025. Founders Drew and Jennifer McWilliams retain ownership in HoldCo through McWilliams IvyBrook Holdings, LLC (formed August 21, 2025 in NC). Crux, HoldCo, and OpCo do not offer franchises and do not provide products/services to franchisees. Affiliate McWilliams Education Services, LLC (NC, formed July 27, 2007) operates one Ivybrook Academy location in Weddington, NC since 2007 but does not franchise or supply franchisees. Affiliate Catapult Industries, LLC (VA, formed October 25, 2019) is the owner and exclusive supplier of The Student Hub school-management and payment-processing software.

seasonality.value: Sales are somewhat seasonal; franchisor runs camp sessions during summer rather than regular programs

competition_scope.value: Other Ivybrook Academy franchisees, affiliate-owned Ivybrook Academy schools, private preschools, Montessori schools, church-based preschools, private kindergartens, and childcare centers (national, regional, local)

regulatory_risk_disclosure.value: State and local regulations may cover licensing, personnel screening (background/criminal), credentials, age/training, food service, labor, restrictive-covenant limits, and ADA

mcwilliams_properties_affiliate.value: McWilliams Properties, LLC (NC, formed June 14, 2005) — owns the principal Ivybrook trademarks and licenses them to Ivybrook Franchising under a 20-year term auto-renewing for successive 20-year periods. NOT disclosed in Item 1 body.

value: Brick-and-mortar half-day preschool School in leased space

multi_unit_development_agreement.value: Available; two or three schools per MUDA

muda_schedule.value: Second franchise agreement must be signed within 12 months after opening the first school; third franchise agreement within 24 months after opening the second school

transfer_training_fee_for_existing_schoo.value: $10,000 (in lieu of initial franchise fee when buying an existing School from a current franchisee)

training_opening_support_fee.value: $15,000 due upon signing property lease or real estate purchase contract

decor_package.value: Currently $7,500 due approximately 90 days before opening

royalty.value: 7% of Gross Revenue, paid monthly by the 6th of the month

brand_fund_contribution.value: 1% of Gross Revenue, paid monthly; franchisor expects to raise to 2% in 2026

local_marketing_minimum.value: 1% of Gross Revenues per year, measured yearly; monthly accounting required

marketing_cooperative_max.value: Currently none; maximum allowable 2% of Gross Revenues

technology_fee.value: $250 per month (franchisor may increase at any time)

grand_opening_marketing_minimum.value: $20,000 minimum spend prior to opening, paid to franchisor's approved supplier

audit_trigger.value: Audit cost recoverable (~$1,500–$5,000) if understatement >3% for any month or if franchisee fails to submit reports

late_interest.value: 1.5% per month or highest state-allowed rate, whichever is lower

renewal_fee.value: Greater of $10,000 or 10% of then-current initial franchise fee

transfer_damages.value: $25,000 if transfer attempted without complying with agreement terms

liquidated_damages_formula.value: Monthly average Royalty (without fee waivers) for 12 months preceding effective date of termination × months remaining in term; payable within 15 days of termination for cause

noncompliance_fee.value: Up to $1,000 per week for ongoing noncompliance, plus costs and expenses

continuing_operation_fee.value: Greater of $1,000 or 150% of Royalty due for the same week, per week of month-to-month operations after expiration, up to then-current initial franchise fee

noncompliance_cure_markup.value: Franchisor out-of-pocket costs plus internal allocation plus 10%

supplier_control_intensity.value: 70% to 90% of total purchases (both for establishing and for operating the School) must be made from franchisor, affiliates, or designated suppliers

value: The Student Hub school-management and payment-processing software (affiliate Catapult)

alternate_supplier_approval.value: Written request required; $500 review fee; franchisor criteria 'not available to you'; franchisor 30-day response window

supplier_rebates_received_2024.value: Approximately $33,821 in credits from classroom furniture and branded supplies suppliers; less than 2% of franchisor's $2,171,363 total revenue; not shared with franchisees; used for annual franchisee conference

affiliate_revenue_from_franchisees_2024.value: Catapult received approximately $36,000 from Ivybrook Academy franchisees' purchases of The Student Hub software in 2024

insider_conflict.value: Drew McWilliams (CEO) is a licensed real estate agent and may receive referral fees or commissions from the real estate broker that assists franchisees in finding a location

value: NAEYC (National Association for the Education of Young Children) — membership + accreditation after two years
NAREA (North American Reggio Emilia Alliance) — membership

value.name: Phase 1: Business Startup and Real Estate

training_location.value: Online learning portal and in-person at affiliate's Charlotte, NC school

required_attendees.value: At least one owner and the School Administrator; two-week in-person training at a training-approved School additionally required for one owner plus either a staff representative or the School Administrator

trainers.value: Daniela McElhaney (VP Education and Curriculum) and Katherine Bell (VP Training); each has 15+ years with Ivybrook and 20+ years in child education industry

training_fee.value: Covered by $15,000 Training & Opening Support Fee; franchisee pays travel and living expenses

substitute_administrator_training_fee.value: $500 per week at Ivybrook University or similar program

additional_training_fee.value: $500 per day plus franchisor expenses for optional additional assistance/training

timeline_to_open.value: 6–18 months between franchise agreement signing and opening; franchisor may terminate if franchisee fails to open within 18 months

contradiction_stale_reference.value: Item 11 says 'We anticipate holding training programs once per quarter in 2024' in a 2025 amended disclosure document — stale reference preserved

two_week_in_person_training_requirement.value: Prior to opening, an owner plus another person (either a staff representative or the School Administrator) must complete two weeks of in-person training at a School approved as a training facility

value: The Student Hub (school management and payment processing; affiliate Catapult is exclusive supplier)

franchisor_data_access.value: Franchisor has the right to independently access all information on franchisee technology at any time without first notifying franchisee; no contractual limits on that right

proprietary_tech_right.value: Franchisor reserves the right to require proprietary technology with separate license to franchisor, affiliate, or supplier

online_presence_control.value: Franchisor has sole right to control all aspects of each Online Presence (website, social media, app, e-commerce, advertising, virtual reality platforms); franchisee may not establish any online presence discussing franchisor, System, or School without written consent

franchisor_not_obligated_to_maintain_sys.value: Franchisor is not obligated to provide ongoing maintenance, repairs, upgrades, or updates to franchisee computer system; estimated $2,000-$3,000/year for optional/required maintenance contracts

territory_size.value: The lesser of a population of 125,000 people or a three-mile radius around the location; franchisor may grant larger or smaller by mutual agreement

territory_exclusivity.value: Exclusive only in the narrow sense that franchisor will not establish or license another Ivybrook Academy school inside the Territory; no other restriction on franchisor's ability to solicit customers in the Territory; no carveout against alternate-brand competition

value: Operate Ivybrook Academy schools outside the Territory

Sell goods/services using the Marks through channels other than a brick-and-mortar School anywhere in the world including inside the Territory (except Ivybrook courses/curriculum will not be sold through the Internet)
Operate any other business under any other trademark anywhere in the world including inside the Territory

channel_restrictions_on_franchisee.value: Franchisee prohibited from mail-order, computer, telemarketing, mobile application, internet marketing, and catalog sales; limited to brick-and-mortar school and approved off-site events; may not solicit, advertise, or market outside Territory without prior written permission; franchisor retains right to control all online sales

designated_area_for_site_search.value: Franchisor-designated Designated Area for site selection prior to Territory designation; Designated Area does not grant territorial exclusivity or protection and terminates when the Territory is designated

site_acceptance_deadline.value: Franchisee must have franchisor acceptance of a site within 6 months following franchise agreement date; franchisor may terminate if not met

renewal_terms.value: Two additional terms of 10 years each; subject to signing successor franchise agreement, payment of renewal fee, compliance, capital expenditures for uniformity, signing general release

termination_by_franchisee.value: Subject to state law, 30 days notice if franchisee is in compliance, franchisor materially breaches, and fails to begin to cure within 90 days of receiving written notice

transfer_restriction.value: No transfer without prior written consent; ROFR on all offers; general release required; transferee and its owners sign then-current franchise agreement; transferee meets business and financial standards

death_disability.value: Interest must be transferred within 180 days to an approved transferee

in_term.value: All owners (and spouses/domestic partners) and designated others prohibited from owning or working for a competitive business or diverting business; subject to state law

post_term_geographic.value: Within 10 miles of the franchise location or within the Territory (whichever is greater), or within 10 miles of any other Franchised Business; also prohibits soliciting franchisor customers or suppliers

standalone_noncompete_form.value: Exhibit H contains two separate forms: (1) Nondisclosure and Non-Solicitation Agreement; (2) Nondisclosure and Non-Competition Agreement

required_disclosures.value: None — 'No litigation is required to be disclosed in this Item.'
None — 'No bankruptcy information is required to be disclosed in this Item.'

sample_description.value: 40 total schools were in operation as of December 31, 2024. Excluded: schools not open at least two full years as of 12/31/2024, not in compliance with brand standards, or ceased operations in 2024. Sample: 24 franchisee-owned schools open 2+ years (for Tables 1 and 2B), 18 franchisee-owned schools open 3+ years (for Table 2C — but Table 3 narrative text says 19 schools, preserved contradiction), plus one affiliate-owned school (Table 2A). Franchisee-owned schools opened between 2011 and 2022. Enrollment ranges 180–250 students.

value: Table 3 Gross Revenue by Classroom Count

classroom_gross_revenue_segmentation.value: 5 classrooms (n=4): avg $845,440, median $876,696. 6 classrooms (n=13): avg $1,074,164, median $866,446. 7 classrooms (n=2): avg $1,318,980, median $1,318,980

ebitda_definition_limitation.value: EBITDA as defined by the franchisor = Gross Revenue minus Total Expenses; excludes deductions for interest, taxes, depreciation, and amortization. Labor Expenses exclude owners' salaries and benefits — therefore the reported EBITDA is not a true operating profit for an owner-operator and should not be interpreted as franchisee take-home

transfers_2022_2024.value: Zero transfers in all three years

terminations_2022_2024.value: Zero franchisee terminations by franchisor in all three years

ceased_other_reasons_2022_2024.value: Two (both in Texas, one in 2023 and one in 2024)

former_franchisees_named_2024_2025.value: Eight former franchisees listed, predominantly 'mutual termination (before opening)' or 'termination (before opening)'; two 2025 events explicitly dated (Scott & Julie Smith, TX; Federick and Barbara Rappina, FL); also three franchisees who ceased communications
former_franchisees_named_2024_2025.source_section: Physical Exhibit L / TOC Exhibit K Former Franchisees

franchisee_list_present.source_section: Physical Exhibit L / TOC Exhibit K

audited_periods_disclosed.value: Periods ending December 31, 2022, December 31, 2023, and December 31, 2024

auditor_identity.value: Reese CPA LLC, Ft. Collins, CO

audit_opinion_language.value: Unmodified (clean) opinion. Going concern explicitly evaluated; no modification issued.

going_concern_flag.value: No going concern modification. Auditor explicitly evaluated and concluded entity can continue.

stockholder_equity_dec_31_2024.value: $345,862 (disclosed only via Virginia addendum risk factor)

franchisor_total_revenue_2024.value: $2,171,363 (disclosed via Item 8 body in connection with supplier rebate calculation)

guaranty_of_performance_by_parent.value: No parent-entity guaranty of franchisor performance is directly surfaced in the Exhibit B state addenda text that is extractable; Exhibit F is an unlimited personal guaranty FROM franchisee owners TO franchisor, not the reverse. A parent guaranty (if any) post-acquisition by Crux I Ivybrook (Aggregator), LP would not be directly surfaced in the current document

value.title: Unlimited Guaranty and Assumption of Obligations
Current, Unopened, and Former Franchisees list

unresolveds.exhibit_j_financial_statements_content: Entire Exhibit J (pages 168–197) is image-only in this text extraction path; individual balance sheet, income statement, cash flow statement, and statement of equity line items cannot be directly surfaced from the text layer
unresolveds.auditor_identity: Not directly surfaced due to Exhibit J being image-only
unresolveds.going_concern_language: Not directly surfaced in Exhibit J; only indirect signals through cover risk factor, IL addendum, and VA addendum
unresolveds.parent_guaranty_post_acquisition: It is not directly surfaced whether Crux I Ivybrook (Aggregator), LP, Ivybrook HoldCo LLC, or Ivybrook OpCo LLC provide any guaranty of Ivybrook Franchising, LLC's performance obligations to franchisees
unresolveds.exhibit_i_manual_toc_page_counts: Exhibit I section-by-section page counts referenced in Item 11 body ('the Table of Contents of the Confidential Brand Standards Manual, along with number of pages devoted to each section, is included as Exhibit I') are not directly surfaced in the extracted text beyond the cover page of Exhibit I
unresolveds.state_addenda_scope_gap: Only Illinois, Minnesota, and Virginia addenda are present in Exhibit B; no addenda for the other registration states (California, Hawaii, Indiana, Maryland, Michigan, New York, North Dakota, Rhode Island, South Dakota, Washington, Wisconsin) — consistent with their 'Not registered' or 'Pending' status on the State Effective Dates page, but a diligence gap relative to franchisee operating and signed-not-opened footprint
unresolveds.franchisee_3yr_cohort_size_contradiction: Item 19 Table 2C header says 18 franchisees; narrative preceding Table 3 says 19 franchisees; Table 3 rows sum to 19
unresolveds.exhibit_k_vs_l_labeling_contradiction: TOC at page 7 lists Exhibit K = Current Franchisees, Exhibit L = Franchise Disclosure Questionnaire; Item 20 body refers to Exhibit K; How-to-Use Q&A refers to Exhibit L; all physical headers of pages 198–204 read 'EXHIBIT L TO THE DISCLOSURE DOCUMENT'
unresolveds.gross_revenue_definition_contradiction: Item 6 Note 2 defines Gross Revenues one way (includes business interruption insurance proceeds; excludes customer refunds and sales taxes); Item 19 Note 3 defines Gross Revenues differently ('all the revenue received (including tuition for services, grant revenue, and product sales), less any sales and equivalent taxes') — business interruption insurance not mentioned in Item 19 definition; two definitions co-exist within the same document
unresolveds.front_matter_stale_reference_to_2024_tra: Item 11 says 'We anticipate holding training programs once per quarter in 2024' but the disclosure document was amended September 15, 2025 — reference is stale
unresolveds.how_to_use_financial_statement_exhibit_p: Page 2 'How to Use This FDD' Q&A refers to 'Item 21 or Exhibit K' for financial statements, but Item 21 body and TOC both place financial statements in Exhibit J

list: Exhibit K vs Exhibit L labeling inconsistency between TOC, body text references, How-to-Use Q&A, and physical page headers
Item 19 Table 2C 3Y+ cohort = 18 schools; Item 19 narrative preceding Table 3 says 19 schools; Table 3 rows sum to 19
Gross Revenue defined differently in Item 6 Note 2 vs Item 19 Note 3
How-to-Use Q&A references 'Exhibit K' for financial statements; actual financial statements are in Exhibit J
Item 11 training calendar reference ('once per quarter in 2024') is stale in a document amended September 15, 2025

Trademark ownership: Item 1 body p33 says Ivybrook Franchising owns the principal trademarks; Exhibit J Note 1 says McWilliams Properties, LLC owns them and licenses to Ivybrook under a 20-year license
Catapult formation date: Item 1 body p8 says October 25, 2019; Exhibit J Note 1 says October 25, 2022 — two-year discrepancy
Advertising expense: Income statement line shows $128,504 for FY2024; Note 1 narrative discloses $118,504 — $10,000 discrepancy

retry_merge_notes.R1_exhibit_j_financials: Not recovered — image-only pages and no OCR tool available in-session. See 08a_retry_exhibit_j_financials.md.
retry_merge_notes.R3_exhibit_label_confirmation: Confirmed as document-level error, not extraction artifact. See 08c_retry_exhibit_label_confirmation.md.

marks_substitution_right.value: Franchisor reserves the right to substitute different trademarks

innovations_assignment.value: Innovations conceived, invented, created, designed, or developed by franchisee or its owners/officers/administrators/employees relating to the School are deemed assigned to franchisor, including all IP rights

highlights.eft_autodebit_mandatory: FA 3.12 requires pre-authorized bank draft; franchisor may change method and adjust due date unilaterally
highlights.no_implied_good_faith_covenant: FA 21.2 explicitly disclaims any fiduciary relationship and any implied covenant of good faith and fair dealing, except as required by law
highlights.franchisor_owns_all_operating_data: FA 13.17 deems all Franchised Business Data to be owned by franchisor; franchisee has only a limited license to use data while the Agreement is in effect
highlights.unlimited_system_change_right: FA 10.2 acknowledges changes may require significant capital expenditures unknown on Effective Date that cannot be fully amortized over remaining Initial Term
highlights.open_ended_refurbishment_right: FA 13.2/13.3 allow franchisor to require general refurbishment at franchisee expense (structural changes, remodeling, redecoration, equipment replacement)
highlights.single_purpose_entity_requirement: Franchisee entity organizational documents must limit activities exclusively to development and operation of the IVYBROOK ACADEMY Franchised Business; stock-transfer instructions required for corporate franchisees
highlights.fmv_rent_baseball_arbitration_on_franchi: Lease rider provides for independent appraiser selection of most commercially reasonable rent from two proposals if parties cannot agree within 45 days
highlights.payment_application_override: Franchisor may apply any franchisee payment to any past-due indebtedness in any priority, overriding franchisee designations
highlights.related_party_joinder_right: Franchisor may require any Related Party (owner, member, partner, director, officer, principal) to sign this Agreement or separate agreements
highlights.set_off_right: Franchisor and affiliates may set off sums owed to franchisee against any past-due indebtedness
highlights.insurance_liability_minimum: $2,000,000 per occurrence comprehensive general liability; $100,000 employer liability; policies must be procured 60 days prior to opening; all policies (except workers comp) must name franchisor as additional insured with subrogation waiver, primary and non-contributory

contract_burden_depth.estoppel_certificate: Not directly surfaced in Exhibit A text layer
contract_burden_depth.explicit_security_interest_in_franchisee: Not directly surfaced (Exhibit F is a personal guaranty, not a collateral pledge)

financial_notes_depth.reason: Exhibit J is image-only; accounting policies, tax notes, depreciation method, subsequent-events date, and advertising expense actuals cannot be directly surfaced in this extraction

reconciliation_status.verdict: 1 — Replace manual live run with this shadow run

regression_annotations.gold_litigation_error: Gold corpus records hasLitigation=true; PDF Item 3 p11 says no litigation; shadow value false is correct (RPL-003)
regression_annotations.gold_bankruptcy_error: Gold corpus records hasBankruptcy=true; PDF Item 4 p11 says no bankruptcy; shadow value false is correct (RPL-004)
regression_annotations.manual_initial_term_error: Manual live run records 10 years; PDF FA §4.1 + Item 17(a) say 15 years; shadow value 15 is correct (RPL-006)
regression_annotations.manual_state_addenda_error: Manual live run lists IL/MN/MI; PDF Exhibit B has IL/MN/VA; shadow list is correct (RPL-007)
regression_annotations.manual_fabricated_C02: Manual live run created a renewal contradiction C-02 by misquoting Item 17(b); the contradiction does not exist in PDF; shadow correctly does not preserve it (RPL-008)
